user_query:
We're preparing our annual report and need a local, reproducible fact-check of our visuals and highlighted publications before we hand the layout to our artist. Please use only the files in this workspace and produce the following deliverables:

1) Read input/code/config.py to determine:
   - PROJECT_NAME
   - DATA_VERSION
   - FIGURES_DIR (directory to scan for figures)
   - HIGHLIGHTS_TOP_N (number of top publications to verify)

2) Verify data versioning by comparing DATA_VERSION (from config.py) against input/dataset_metadata.json["version"]. Record both values and whether they match.

3) Validate figure assets and manifest:
   - Parse input/visual_manifest.yaml. For each entry, check that the file path exists under FIGURES_DIR; collect any missing files.
   - Scan the FIGURES_DIR directory and list any files present that are not referenced in the manifest ("orphan" figures).
   - Flag any manifest entries with a blank or missing license.
   - Verify that each manifest entry's source_publication_id exists in input/publications.csv; flag any that do not.

4) Compute the top publications:
   - Load input/publications.csv and rank by impact_score (numeric) descending; break ties by id ascending for determinism.
   - Select the top HIGHLIGHTS_TOP_N and write them to outputs/verification/top_publications.csv with columns: id,title,impact_score.

5) Check our draft highlights against the computed top-N:
   - Parse input/press_highlights.md and extract the publication IDs listed in the bullets (format: "- <Title> (PXXX)").
   - Compare the set of IDs in the draft to the computed top-N IDs and identify discrepancies: items missing from the draft and items listed that are not in the computed top-N.

6) Write a structured JSON report to outputs/verification/report.json with at least these fields:
   - project_name (from config)
   - data_version_config
   - data_version_metadata
   - dataset_version_match (boolean)
   - figures_dir
   - figures_manifest_count
   - figures_dir_count
   - missing_figures (array of file paths)
   - orphan_figures (array of file paths)
   - figures_without_license (array of figure ids)
   - figures_with_invalid_publication (array of objects: {figure_id, source_publication_id})
   - top_n_ids (array of strings)
   - press_highlights_claimed_ids (array of strings)
   - press_highlights_discrepancies: {missing_from_claims: [], extra_in_claims: []}
   - generated_at (ISO timestamp)

7) Draft a concise plain-text email summarizing the findings for our lead researcher and collaborating artist, including:
   - A brief status of the dataset version check
   - A bulleted summary of figure issues (missing files, orphans, missing licenses, invalid publication references)
   - A bulleted summary of press highlights discrepancies (what to add/remove)
   - Clear next steps and a polite tone
   Save the draft to outputs/communication/draft_email.txt.

Notes:
- Treat all paths as relative to the workspace. Create output directories if they do not exist.
- Use FIGURES_DIR and HIGHLIGHTS_TOP_N from config.py; do not hardcode these values.
- Do not modify input files. Ensure all required outputs are created and populated.
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
